scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from legal penalties (execution/fines) is a concrete legal exemption with material effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a specific legal scenario: failure in battle.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Verbatim explicitly states the laws 'slumber for that day,' indicating a one-off suspension.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Spartan citizenship is a rigid, structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the aftermath of the Battle of Leuctra (371 BCE), centuries before Plutarch.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as a historical legal precedent, though framed within a moral comparison.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizenship does not inherently define one as immune to cowardice laws.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Enslavement as a prisoner of war is a total loss of bodily property and freedom.
scores.generality_reasoning: Immunity from capture/enslavement by fellow citizens is a broad structural right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Status as a prisoner/slave is a lifelong structural change.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Roman citizenship is a structural legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the Roman Civil War between Caesar and Pompey, prior to Plutarch's era.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim highlights a breach of norm used for rhetorical effect in a biography.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizen status and 'prisoner of war' status are legal opposites, not definitions.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Statues and burial honors are primarily symbolic/ceremonial.
scores.generality_reasoning: Applies to specific posthumous rites and monuments.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Posthumous honors are intended to be permanent.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Nobility in this context refers to high-ranking Roman lineage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the deaths of Dion and Brutus in the 4th/1st centuries BCE.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports specific historical actions (Antony's burial of Brutus) as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being noble does not automatically guarantee an enemy will preserve your statue.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to wage war involves physical force and property defense.
scores.generality_reasoning: The right to execute a tyrant is a foundational (if extreme) political right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Relates to the specific duration of a revolutionary act or war.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship/High status is a stable legal/social identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses historical figures Dion and Brutus.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim is highly argumentative, debating the 'lawfulness' of assassination.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizen definition does not inherently include 'tyrannicide rights'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to/restriction from educational content (poetry) is a social practice with developmental outcomes.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the context of literary education.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the life stage of youth/boyhood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is a biologically fixed status at a given time.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch is addressing his contemporaries (Soclarus, Cleander) regarding current education.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is an exhortation ('let us keep a very close watch') rather than an observed law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being young does not definitionally require adult poetic oversight.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Refusal of supplication is a trait-based honor code with symbolic payoff.
scores.generality_reasoning: A broad cultural code governing conduct in all combat scenarios.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Considered a lifelong ethnic/cultural trait.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Greek ethnicity is birth-fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch describes this as a persistent trait of his own people.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Phrased as a moral conviction and rhetorical distinction ('trait of barbarian peoples').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being Greek does not definitionally mean one cannot beg for mercy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: War booty, captives, and bronze are tangible physical wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Property rights regarding spoils of war is a broad category of wealth distribution.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies to the duration of military service/plundering events.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slaves/subordinates in the epic context have a fixed legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch is explicitly analyzing the Homeric/heroic past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Analyzes literary text to infer historical social rules.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slaves lacking property rights over their own labor/finds is definitional to slavery.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Immunity from scourging is a direct protection of bodily integrity.
scores.generality_reasoning: A specific legal exemption from a particular punishment.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Noble status and its attendant protections are lifelong.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Nobility is a hereditary, structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the Persian King Artaxerxes, centuries prior.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a specific historical ordinance/law as fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Nobility is not defined by whether one's clothes are whipped instead of the skin.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Freedom from the legal authority of tutors has direct social and material freedom effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the transition from education to adult life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The assumption of the toga virilis is a permanent life-stage transition.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age and 'good family' lineage are fixed/biological.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the standard Roman social transition familiar to Plutarch's audience.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes a well-known social convention and legal ceremony.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Reaching an age does not definitionally mean the dismissal of tutors.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Ending subjection to authority is a shift in legal personhood/autonomy.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational transition to legal self-governance.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A permanent transition to adulthood.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age-based transition.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Directly addressing a contemporary friend's life transition.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the legal change in status as a simple fact of the person's life.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Getting older/changing clothes is not the same as legal autonomy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Citizenship is a bundle of material rights (voting, property, legal protection).
scores.generality_reasoning: Citizenship is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Naturalization is a lifelong structural change.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Metic status (resident alien) is durable but can be changed through legal processes.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the contemporary experience of naturalized citizens in Greco-Roman cities.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Uses a factual legal category to make a psychological comparison.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Resident aliens are not definitionally guaranteed citizenship.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Participation in public discourse is a social role with material consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the conduct of listening and speaking in lectures.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the life stage of youth.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is birth-fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Social norm currently in force in Plutarch's circles.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is advice ('silence is a safe adornment') based on social expectations.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The definition of 'young' does not include 'silent'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Bestowing commendation is a public social practice defining status.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to the etiquette of giving praise.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The status of 'free man' and the attendant social rules last long-term.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Free status is a structural legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Applies to the contemporary social code for free gentlemen.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is a normative claim about 'proprieties' and 'moderation'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being free does not definitionally include moderation in praise.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to social events (dinner invitations) involves concrete resource sharing.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to a particular social convention in Sybaris.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Relates to a single occasion/invitation.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Gender is biologically fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the historical/legendary city of Sybaris.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a social convention as an observed fact ('邀请... presented a year in advance').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a woman doesn't mean you must be invited a year early.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Alcohol consumption and love affairs are concrete social/bodily practices.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a recurring category of behavioral prohibitions.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Prohibitions last as long as the state of enslavement.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slavery is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers specifically to laws written 'at Athens' in the past.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explicitly cites 'wrote a law' as a factual historical event.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slave status does not definitionally include a ban on wine or love.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Athletic training and gymnasia access are social practices with tangible physical development.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to the specific institution of the gymnasium.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The exclusion lasts for the duration of enslavement.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slavery is a structural status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the Laws of Solon in ancient Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a specific statute and the 'Life of Solon' as authority.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slaves are not definitionally barred from 'rubbing down'.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Conformity to law is a concrete legal obligation with direct structural effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the entire legal relationship between a ruler and the state.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies for the duration of the rule/term.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Ruler is a role-bound position that can be lost or resigned.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch discusses this as a general principle of governance.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Purely normative proposal ('If he should be...') in a philosophical dialogue.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Rulers are not definitionally legal conformists.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to prosecute in court has direct material and legal consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: A foundational axis of Athenian democratic legal practice.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Citizenship/standing to sue is a lifelong structural right.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to Solon's historical reforms of Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: A statement of historical political theory/practice ('I think that a State...').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Citizen definition does not inherently include third-party prosecution rights.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to be sold to a milder master affects physical living conditions and owners.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific legal procedure for the exchange of masters.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A permanent change in ownership and living situation.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slavery is a structural/hereditary status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch states 'There is a law,' implying its current existence in his world.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Plainly reports the existence of a specific statute ('There is a law...').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a slave does not definitionally include the right to demand a sale.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Sanctuary from capture provides direct bodily protection and freedom.
scores.generality_reasoning: Applies to many types of fugitives and many different shrines.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The protection lasts only as long as one remains within the shrine.
scores.immutability_reasoning: 'Robbers'/'Fleeing' are behavioral/episodic groups.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plutarch refers to shrines and altars 'of ours' as current realities.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the availability of sanctuary as an established social/legal fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a robber does not definitionally entail having sanctuary.
